9.reason: [Incorrect cast: BTree is already a pointer type (BTNode*), so casting malloc to (BTree *) results in a double pointer type (BTNode**), causing a type mismatch. Should be (BTree) or no cast in C.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect cast: (BTree *) should be (BTNode *) or just (struct BTNode *); BTree is already a pointer type, so casting to BTree* results in double pointer.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Incorrect cast: 'BTree' is already a pointer type (BTNode*), so casting malloc to 'BTree*' results in BTNode**, which is wrong. Should be (BTree) or (BTNode*).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect cast: (BTree *) should be (BTNode *) or simply omitted in C; BTree is already a pointer type (BTNode*), so casting to BTree* results in double pointer.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Incorrect cast: (BTree *) should be (BTNode *); BTree is already a pointer type (i.e., BTNode*), so casting to BTree* results in BTNode** which is wrong]

19.reason: [BST insertion logic error: problem requires right child for values >= root, but this only inserts right when x > root->data, ignoring equal values. Equal values should go to the right subtree per problem description.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [BST insertion should handle duplicates by inserting them to the right (>=), but this code only inserts strictly greater values to the right, violating the problem's requirement that right child is >= root.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [BST insertion should handle 'x >= p->data' for right subtree per problem description, but this only inserts when 'x > p->data', causing equal values to be ignored.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [BST insertion should handle duplicates by inserting them to the right (>=), but this code only inserts when x > p->data, ignoring equal values (they are dropped).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [BST insertion should allow duplicates on the right (>=), but this uses only >, so equal values are ignored and not inserted]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [BST insertion logic error: problem requires right child for values >= root, but this only inserts right when x > root->data, ignoring equal values. Equal values should go to the right subtree per problem description.]

29.reason: [Incorrect height tracking: 'high' is a global variable modified during traversal, which does not correctly represent the current node's depth. Height should be passed as a parameter like in the reference program.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Uses global 'high' which does not reflect the actual height of the current leaf node due to flawed increment/decrement logic and shared state across recursive calls.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Printing global 'high' instead of the actual height of the current leaf node. The height should be tracked via recursion parameter, not a global variable.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Uses global 'high' which does not reflect the true height of the current node; also, traversal order is not left-to-right for leaves due to incorrect placement of print before recursive calls.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Uses global 'high' which does not reflect the true height/level of the current node; height must be tracked per call stack, not globally]
